nativeRange.habitat: Shallow coastal waters including mangrove swamps, inlet waters, estuaries, and lagoons with mud, sand, or rock substrates; tends to form surface aggregations

diet.notes: Generally accepts frozen mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, and other meaty marine foods. Has been reported to accept enriched live brine shrimp and glass shrimp. Color may fade if not offered a sufficiently varied and nutritious diet; target feeding twice daily is generally recommended.

summary: A tiny, peaceful schooling goby from the Indo-West Pacific known for hovering in small aggregations near the surface. Generally considered reef-safe and suitable for nano and small reef aquariums with a tightly sealed lid.
careNotes:
Rao's Dart Goby (also sold as Rao's Hover Goby) is a diminutive species generally reaching around 3–4 cm, making it well-suited for smaller reef setups. It has been known to jump, so a tightly fitting aquarium lid is strongly advised. Groups or pairs tend to do better than single specimens; individuals added to an established tank simultaneously have been reported to settle in more readily. A well-established aquarium with abundant live rock, caves, and crevices is generally recommended to provide shelter and supplemental food. Diet color and condition have been noted to deteriorate without a sufficiently varied, nutrition-rich menu of meaty foods.

Temperature note: hobby sources generally advise 22–26°C (72–78°F), while FishBase environmental modeling records the species in 26.4–29.3°C waters in the wild. The averaged range of 24.0–27.5°C is used here; flagged for reviewer verification. This species inhabits both marine and brackish environments in the wild (mangroves, estuaries), though aquarium recommendations target normal reef salinity (SG 1.020–1.025).

Family note: LiveAquaria lists the family as Ptereleotridae; FishBase places the species in Microdesmidae (subfamily Ptereleotrinae), the current accepted taxonomy — Microdesmidae is used here.
breedingNotes: FishBase records external fertilization with batch spawning, and males are reported to exhibit nest-guarding behavior. Captive breeding has not been widely documented in the hobby literature.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Specific species product page; lists care level as Easy, min tank 10 gal, temperature 72–78°F, SG 1.020–1.025, pH 8.1–8.4, dKH 8–12.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirms scientific name, family (Microdesmidae), order (Gobiiformes), max size 3.5 cm SL, wild temperature range 26.4–29.3°C (modeled), Indo-West Pacific distribution, habitat in mangroves and coastal shallows. Temperature disagreement with hobby sources: FishBase wild range is 26.4–29.3°C vs hobby 22.2–25.6°C (72–78°F); averaged to 24.0–27.5°C.

additional.name: Quality Marine
additional.notes: Lists care level as Moderate (vs LiveAquaria Easy); confirms same water parameters and reef-safe status. Care level averaged to intermediate. Notes jump risk and need for tight-fitting lid. Emphasizes enriched/live foods for diet quality.

fish.breedingNotes: FishBase records external fertilization with batch spawning and male nest-guarding behavior. Captive breeding is not widely documented.
